title: feat(ibg-repository): descriptive repository execution planning (IBG-1 PR E)
intent: Introduce a deterministic, DESCRIPTIVE execution-planning layer that transforms an approved RepositoryChangeProposal into a RepositoryExecutionPlan describing how a human engineer could organize the work. Every field is derived from data the proposal and its CBSP21 packet already declare (verification commands, declared files, authorized target paths, declared risk, provenance); nothing is invented. The plan is content-addressed (rep- id), byte-stable for identical proposals, and preserves the proposal's provenance exactly. It is a SEPARATE downstream consumer of the merged proposal contract: it does not modify, wrap, or attach to the PR-D RepositoryProposalPipeline. It terminates at the same human-review boundary and performs no git/filesystem/GitHub/network operation and exercises no repository or canonical authority.
behavior_change: Adds two additive modules (repository_execution_plan.py contract + execution_planner.py builder) plus their tests under the existing services/api/app/ibg_repository/ package, and additively re-exports the PR-E API from __init__.py. No existing runtime path imports the planner; no existing behavior, schema, authority, or governance changes. The planner derives every field mechanically: recommended_validation_sequence is copied verbatim from the packet's verification.commands_run; recommended_commit_sequence / recommended_review_order / structural_dependency_groups are deterministic groupings of declared files by authorized target-path prefix (labelled declared_path_grouping, never a proven dependency edge); estimated_complexity is a documented table over declared counts + declared risk_level; provenance_reference preserves the four evidence-owned fields exactly; planning_summary is a fact-only template. Fail-closed on non-proposal input, empty proposal id, empty/boundary-violating changed files, CBSP21-scope/summary disagreement, missing provenance, or unsupported risk level. An AST-precise test forbids the modules from importing subprocess/os/shutil/tempfile/network/GitHub clients or the PR-B git_runner and worktree machinery.

diff_articulation.what_changed: Creates repository_execution_plan.py: the frozen, content-addressed RepositoryExecutionPlan dataclass plus its typed frozen child records (ExecutionGroup, DependencyGroup, ComplexitySummary, ProvenanceReference), with to_canonical_dict()/to_audit_dict()/compute_plan_hash() and an execution_plan_id derived as 'rep-' + sha256(canonical_content)[:16] (created_at excluded from identity and canonical form). Creates execution_planner.py: build_execution_plan(proposal), the stateless ExecutionPlanner (single 'plan' operation), execution_plan_builder() factory, validate_execution_plan(), the documented complexity table, and the SUPPORTED_RISK_LEVELS set. __init__ additively re-exports the PR-E API. Three test modules bind (planner invariants, acceptance boundary, and contract serialization — the planner tests are split across two modules so each stays under the 500-line file-size gate): stable/byte-identical id + serialization, audit-timestamp exclusion, provenance preserved exactly, source authority unchanged, proposal object unmutated, validation sequence copied without invention, deterministic path grouping (most-specific prefix wins), no synthetic dependency edges (declared_path_grouping only; no dependency_graph attribute), deterministic complexity classification across the documented table, all fail-closed rejects, validate_execution_plan checks, and an AST-precise invariant that neither module imports subprocess/os/shutil/tempfile/requests/httpx/urllib/socket/http/github or the PR-B git_runner/worktree_* machinery. New-module coverage is 100%. The descriptive architecture doc is added under docs/architecture.
diff_articulation.why_not_redundant: No descriptive execution-planning layer existed. PR A-D shipped the proposal, worktree, review-bundle, and thin orchestration contracts, terminating at the human-review boundary with a review artifact; nothing described how an approved proposal could be organized for a human to execute. This layer composes the existing merged proposal contract and derives only structure the proposal already declares — it invents no ordering, dependency edges, or effort, duplicates no proposal/review logic, and does not modify or attach to the PR-D pipeline. It preserves provenance and the human-review boundary, adding a distinct downstream capability rather than re-implementing an existing one.

commands_run: python -m pytest tests/ibg_repository/test_execution_planner.py tests/ibg_repository/test_execution_planner_validation.py tests/ibg_repository/test_repository_execution_plan.py -q --cov=app.ibg_repository.execution_planner --cov=app.ibg_repository.repository_execution_plan --cov-report=term-missing  ->  100% statement AND branch on both modules
python -m pytest tests/ibg_repository/ --no-cov -q  ->  358 passed (PR A + B + C + D + E together, no regressions)
python -m app.ci.check_file_sizes --baseline app/ci/file_sizes_baseline.json  ->  no violations (both planner test modules under the 500-line threshold)

files.intent: Additively re-export the PR-E execution-planning API.
Frozen, content-addressed RepositoryExecutionPlan contract + typed child records.
Planner invariant + boundary tests (derivation, determinism, fail-closed rejects).
Acceptance-boundary tests: scope agreement by file set, plan identity, validate_execution_plan invariants.
Per-PR CBSP21 manifest for PR E.

architecture_scan.acknowledgement_notes: Additive, descriptive-only. Derives every field from the already-merged proposal + CBSP21 packet; invents no ordering/dependency/effort. Separate downstream consumer — does not modify or attach to the PR-D pipeline. No git/filesystem/GitHub/network/authority. Terminates at the human-review boundary. Any execution engine (PR F) remains out of scope.
